items.title: Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel

children.content: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers in Perth reporting a surge in sales.

gallery.altText: Sam smiles in a bike shop.
A close-up shot of a row of e-bikes in a cycle shop, with a white price and specs tag attached to one.
A row of e-bikes outside a cycle shop in Perth, with a fuel price board at a petrol station in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Sam smiles in a bike shop.
A close-up shot of a row of e-bikes in a cycle shop, with a white price and specs tag attached to one.
A row of e-bikes outside a cycle shop in Perth, with a fuel price board at a petrol station in the background.

items.title: All WA regional stations must report fuel prices from May as FuelWatch scheme expands

children.content: The FuelWatch reporting scheme is being expanded to all regional fuel stations in Western Australia, with about 200 additional retailers required to report their fuel prices.

image.altText: Fuel pumps and screens depicting prices for petrol and diesal at a service station.

items.title: Travellers celebrate as major highway reopens after Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Holiday makers took a gamble heading to WA's north hoping a major highway would reopen in time for Easter.

image.altText: Four men play cricket on a road with a boat and caravan parked nearby.

items.title: WA's record for heaviest pumpkin broken

children.content: A pumpkin grown in Albany has beaten the previous WA record for heaviest pumpkin by nearly 60 kilograms.

gallery.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck
A forklift picking up a pumpkin
A medium shot of two sheep eating pumpkin

thumbnail.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck
A forklift picking up a pumpkin
A medium shot of two sheep eating pumpkin

items.title: WA's unusual 'Easter eggs' captured in aerial images

children.content: Scott McCook spends his spare time photographing the salt lakes of WA's Wheatbelt, which look uncannily like giant eggs when viewed from the air.

gallery.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.
Composite: A pink lake surrounded by crop land. A red red lake framed by trees and pale farm land.

thumbnail.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.
Composite: A pink lake surrounded by crop land. A red red lake framed by trees and pale farm land.

items.title: Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'

children.content: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

image.altText: External shots of a 7/11 petrol station and fuel pump with an out of order sign.

items.title: UFOs over the oval: The sighting school kids were told not to talk about

children.content: Flying saucers, balloons or mass hysteria? It's 60 years today since Australia's biggest UFO sighting took place at a Melbourne school. Witnesses are still demanding answers about what happened that day.

image.altText: A woman with short grey hair wearing a red top focus of graphics design image with 'flyer saucer mystery' newspaper behind

items.title: Angry customers who lost $10m in Zone RV collapse urge ASIC to investigate

children.content: With retirement dreams dashed, customers launch a nationwide campaign for the corporate watchdog to investigate the caravan company's former director over insolvent trading claims.

items.title: How one extra tree too close to your house could cost you thousands

children.content: Tree canopy coverage is being boosted in every capital city, but one micro-level analysis has found a link between property prices and the proximity of trees to houses.

image.altText: Dark SUV driving down city street with lots of trees in it

items.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree

children.content: While time is retirement's great gift, it can become a curse for anyone who has devoted decades to a career and then suddenly wakes up with relevance deprivation syndrome and no firm plan on what comes next, writes Matt Eaton.

gallery.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.
A man stands and looks back at the camera with Whitby Abbey in the background, green grass and blue sky.

thumbnail.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.
A man stands and looks back at the camera with Whitby Abbey in the background, green grass and blue sky.

items.title: The cleanest air on Earth holds a powerful lesson about our climate

children.content: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

image.altText: Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

items.title: Moved into a dirty rental? Here's what you need to know

children.content: Having to clean a new rental property before settling in is a common experience for renters, but landlords have obligations when it comes to rental cleanliness upon moving in.

image.altText: A wide-angle image of a clean and modern apartment with large windows, white kitchen cupboards and wooden floors

items.title: Lomax's false start adds to anticipation ahead of pro rugby debut

children.content: Fans will have to wait for Zac Lomax's Western Force debut, providing further intrigue about how the ex-NRL star will cope when he takes his first steps in professional rugby.

gallery.altText: Zac Lomax walking across Lang Park before the Force played the Reds.

thumbnail.altText: Zac Lomax walking across Lang Park before the Force played the Reds.

items.title: Quiz: How well do you know your history?

children.content: Where was the Venus de Milo found? And what common household item first went on sale in April 1827?

image.altText: photo of Venus de Milo statue

items.title: 'Easter bilby' populations booming across sanctuaries

children.content: The Australian Wildlife Conservancy says the threatened species has rebounded at multiple sites across the country.

image.altText: A small marsupial with its ears stuck upwards, standing on red dirt

items.title: Japan assures Australia will get 'normal supply' of fuel

children.content: Fuel will continue to flow from Japan into Australia under assurances given during recent talks between the two countries.

image.altText: A woman in a blue suit laughs as she speaks into a microphone with a Japanese flag behind her.

items.title: Kings break NBL trophy after dramatic overtime win as 36ers blow late lead

children.content: After trailing by six points late in regulation time, a dominant overtime display has earned the Sydney Kings a 113-101 victory in the fifth and deciding match of the NBL Finals.

image.altText: Sydney Kings player Torrey Craig holds two figurines of basketball players after they fell off the NBL championship trophy.

items.title: Cyclone Narelle's destructive path across Australia, in pictures and video

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

gallery.altText: A man looks downcast as he looks at crops
Palm trees in the foreground with a red sky behind.
People look out at the coast as clouds gather.

thumbnail.altText: A man looks downcast as he looks at crops
Palm trees in the foreground with a red sky behind.
People look out at the coast as clouds gather.

items.title: Police launch full-scale search to locate woman missing in popular tourism spot

children.content: A large-scale search is underway for 51-year-old Naomi Marie Duckett, who was last seen at Gap Natural Bridge within Torndirrup National Park just before midday on Thursday.

image.altText: A head shot of a 51-year-old woman with black top and grey eyes.

items.title: Woman charged with murder following investigation into death of 24yo

children.content: The woman, 48, and the deceased were known to each other, according to Homicide Squad detectives.

image.altText: A shot of a grey building with the words 'central law courts' written on the side.

items.title: Bulldogs down plucky Bombers but lose two more players to injury

children.content: The Western Bulldogs hold off an Essendon fight back to triumph by 34 points, but will sweat on the fitness of Artie Jones and Tim English, who both limped off at Docklands.

image.altText: An AFL player in red and blue is kneeled over in pain

items.title: Resurgent Jason Day back at Masters after career of near misses

children.content: After five top-10 finishes at the Masters, including last year, Australian Jason Day returns to Augusta in hot form as he wrestles with his clubs.

image.altText: Golfer Jason Day leans against a tree during a Masters practice round at Augusta National.

items.title: World-famous Bibbulmun Track considered for heritage list

children.content: Councils support moves for the Bibbulmun Track to be declared a heritage-listed site, which conservationists hope could protect the track from future mining expansions.

Albany coastline with wind farm in background
A suspension bridge with two people on it high above the ground in a bushland setting.
